title: API Key Resource
description: Canonical API key resource returned by POST /api/v1/keys (201 Created) and GET /api/v1/keys/{key_id}. The raw secret value is ONLY present in the create response (create_secret) and is never returned again; subsequent reads omit it. This schema models the create response shape that includes create_secret.
x-privacy-note: create_secret is a one-time secret; never log, cache, or re-transmit. prefix is safe to display and log.

key_id.description: Stable server-assigned identifier for the key.

name.description: Human-readable label set at creation time.

prefix.description: Short public prefix of the key (safe to display and log; does not allow reconstruction of the secret).

project_id.description: Project the key is scoped to, or null for tenant-wide keys.

scopes.description: Space-delimited granted scope tokens. Matches the requested scopes after server validation.

expires_at.description: Expiry timestamp, or null if the key does not expire.

create_secret.description: One-time plaintext secret returned ONLY in the create (201) response. Absent in all subsequent reads. The client MUST store this value immediately; it cannot be retrieved again.
